rationale: The confirmed US-Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening continues as the dominant story affecting Polish energy prices and cost of living — no new qualitative change this cycle, so baseline holds.

top_events.title: US, Iran confirm ceasefire; Strait of Hormuz reopening, markets soar, oil falls
top_events.summary: Stock markets rising and oil prices falling following the confirmed US-Iran deal directly benefits Poland through lower energy costs and reduced inflation pressure. No new qualitative change this cycle — continuation of last cycle's development.

top_events.title: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva
top_events.summary: Anti-G7 protests in Geneva are a distant event with no direct impact on Polish daily life; minor background noise for an EU/NATO member.

top_events.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike
top_events.summary: A US strike on a Venezuelan gang leader has no meaningful direct impact on Poland; distant event with no regional security link.
